text: Testing plays a vital role in software development. Unit tests check small pieces of code, like individual functions, to ensure they work correctly alone. Integration tests go further and verify that different parts of the system work well together. Many developers use test-driven development, a method where you write a failing test before writing the actual code. This approach forces you to think carefully about requirements. Together, these practices build confidence in your software. You can refactor or add new features more easily, knowing that a failing test will quickly alert you to any problems. For professional programmers, testing is not optional; it is essential for quality.
